# @fluenti/vite-plugin

> Vite plugin for Fluenti — build-time i18n template transforms, virtual modules, and per-route code splitting.

Docs: https://fluenti.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/vite-plugin

## Install

```bash
pnpm add -D @fluenti/vite-plugin
```

## Setup

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import fluenti from '@fluenti/vite-plugin'

export default {
  plugins: [
    fluenti({
      framework: 'vue',      // 'vue' | 'react' | 'solid' | 'auto'
      splitting: 'dynamic',  // 'dynamic' | 'static' | 'per-route' | false
    }),
  ],
}
```

## Options

- `framework`: `'vue' | 'react' | 'solid' | 'auto'` (default: `'auto'`)
- `catalogDir`: path to compiled message catalogs (default: `'src/locales/compiled'`)
- `sourceLocale`: source locale (default: `'en'`)
- `locales`: available locales (default: `[sourceLocale]`)
- `splitting`: code splitting strategy (default: `false`)
- `defaultBuildLocale`: locale for static splitting (default: `sourceLocale`)

## Compile-time Transforms

- `v-t` directive becomes `$t()` calls in Vue templates
- `<Trans>` / `<Plural>` become optimized render output
- `t()` function calls become `__i18n.t()` with auto-injected imports
- Rich text with child elements uses `$vtRich()` for catalog-based rendering

## Code Splitting Strategies

- `false`: no splitting (default)
- `'dynamic'`: lazy-load locale catalogs on demand
- `'static'`: single locale inlined at build time, ideal for SSR
- `'per-route'`: messages automatically split by route

## Virtual Modules

- `virtual:fluenti/runtime` — reactive catalog with `__switchLocale()`, `__preloadLocale()`
- `virtual:fluenti/messages` — static re-export for SSR builds
- `virtual:fluenti/route-runtime` — per-route catalog with `__loadRoute()`

## Exports

- `default`: the plugin factory function `fluentiPlugin(options?): Plugin[]`
- `createVtNodeTransform()`: Vue compiler AST transform for `v-t` directive
- `transformSolidJsx(code)`: compile-time JSX transform for Solid `<Trans>` and `<Plural>`
- `FluentiPluginOptions`: TypeScript interface for plugin options
